scene.title: The Wanderer Returns
scene.intro: It has been the better part of a year since {name} walked out of Runehold's gate to follow a thread of old lore into the ash. Tonight the watch calls down: a figure on the road, coming home. But the season is late, the road is long, and the Forgetting takes travellers as surely as it takes workings.

advisors.text: {name} was strong of mind, Archon. If anyone could walk that road and come back {themselves}, it is {them}. Go down and meet {them} — you will know at the gate.
Pray it is {name} who comes through that gate. The road gives back what it took, but not always as it took it.

choices.label: Go down to the gate and meet the traveller

win.text: It is {name} — thinner, wind-burned, older about the eyes, but {them}, whole and knowing you. {They} carr{ies} a satchel of lore worth the year {they} spent, and the hearth is warmer tonight for {their} return.
win.saga: {name} came home from the long road whole and {themselves}, bearing a year's worth of hard-won lore.

lose.text: The figure that stops at the edge of the firelight wears {name}'s face and {name}'s cloak — but the eyes are wrong, and it does not answer to {their} name. The road gave something back. It is not, quite, who set out.
lose.saga: What came home on the long road wore {name}'s face, but the Forgetting had been at {them} — a Soul stood where {name} had been.

scene.title: Name the Returned
scene.intro: The Soul waits at the edge of the firelight to be known. It came home wearing {name}'s face — but the Flame has rewritten what is behind it, and the coven has learned that a returning Soul is not always the one who left. Sometimes it is one of your own, long lost, come back in a borrowed shape. Name it truly, and you give it a kind of peace. Name it wrong, and the Circle will wonder what else you have forgotten.
